claim: Agents perform poorly with >10 tools; optimal range is 5-10 tools per agent before decision-making degrades significantly
actionable: When designing agents, limit tool set to 5-10 maximum; if more needed, split into specialist agents with smaller toolsets

claim: Supervisor architecture: single agent routes to specialist subagents, providing control and reliability vs. unreliable peer-to-peer communication
actionable: Use supervisor pattern for production multi-agent systems; have one agent decide routing rather than agents deciding who calls next

claim: Agent communication via shared state objects or tool-call parameters; tool-call approach is simpler, shared state more flexible
actionable: For simple agent coordination, pass parameters via tool calls; for complex state, use shared message lists or state objects

claim: Most production multi-agent systems use custom cognitive architectures, not off-the-shelf supervisor/hierarchical patterns; tailor to your domain
actionable: Rather than adopting supervisor or hierarchical templates, build custom multi-agent flow tailored to your domain using LangGraph
